statement: Micron says DRAM and NAND supply-demand conditions will remain tight beyond calendar 2027 driven by AI demand and structural supply constraints, with HBM3E/HBM4 effectively sold out through 2027 and order books extending into 2028.
why: Sold-out HBM and tight memory supply cap the volume of datacenter GPUs that can be built, since HBM is a gating component.

impact.mechanism: Tight, sold-out HBM supports memory pricing while constraining merchant-GPU output and lengthening effective availability.

evidence.source: Micron Q3 FY2026 slides via Investing.com
evidence.excerpt: Supply-demand conditions for both DRAM and NAND are expected to remain tight beyond calendar 2027, driven by AI-driven demand across all segments and structural supply constraints.

confidence.basis: Single secondary publisher (Investing.com) reporting company slides; not a primary filing.
